title: campanula scheuchzeri var. carnica
intro: Campanula scheuchzeri var. carnica, also known as Alpine Bellflower, is a perennial plant species in the Campanulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues and reduce fever. The Alpine Bellflower is believed to have various health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help in soothing sore throats and relieving digestive issues. Alpine Bellflower is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusions, and its roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits.
